response: EU new rules under Ecodesign for Sustainable Products Regulation prohibit the destruction of unsold apparel, clothing, accessories and footwear to reduce waste and environmental damage. The regulation mandates companies to disclose information on discarded goods and bans their destruction due to safety or product damage. This helps create a more circular economy by encouraging businesses to manage stock effectively, explore alternatives like resale, remanufacturing, donations, or reuse instead of discarding unsold items. These measures apply from July 2026 for large companies and will be implemented in 2030 for medium-sized companies. The rules aim to make products more durable, reusable, and recyclable while boosting efficiency and competitiveness in the sector.
